description_long: This object is a hand axe featuring a straight, cylindrical wooden handle with a smooth, polished finish and a natural wood grain pattern. The handle is attached to a single, broad, wedge-shaped metal blade that is matte black in color, with a slightly curved cutting edge and a flat poll opposite the edge. The blade is mounted perpendicularly to the handle, forming a classic hatchet or carpenter's axe profile. The transition between the handle and blade is seamless, with the blade fitted securely into the end of the handle. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding. The axe appears robust and suitable for chopping or splitting tasks in urban environments such as parks, gardens, or garages.
description: A robust hand axe with a straight wooden handle and a broad, matte black metal blade, designed for chopping or splitting tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped black blade.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the circular cross-section of the handle and the thin profile of the blade.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle and the flat poll of the blade, with the cutting edge visible.
description_view_3: View from the top of the handle, showing the attachment point and the width of the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical wooden handle with a smooth finish and round cross-section, no visible grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single, broad, wedge-shaped steel blade with a slightly curved cutting edge and flat poll, no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted into the end of the wooden handle.
